name: Formula One 1969
attribution: Historical data derived from f1db (github.com/f1db/f1db, CC BY 4.0)
AI ratings from 'Custom AI by jusk - F1 1969 Season' (F-Vintage_Gen2.xml), matching Alain Fry's F1 1969 skinpack
notes: Coverage-note scope: each 'entrant ... not included' line below refers to that exact f1db constructor/engine/round stint, not necessarily to the named driver. The full-base roster may represent that driver through a different source livery while still omitting the exact historical car combination.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Kurt Ahrens, Jr. (brabham+ford, rounds 7) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Vic Elford (cooper+maserati, rounds 3) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Dieter Quester (bmw+bmw, rounds 7) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Hubert Hahne (bmw+bmw, rounds 7) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Peter Westbury (brabham+ford, rounds 7) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.

Coverage: Richard Attwood's round-3 Lotus-Ford stint has no matching livery and remains omitted. His source-authored Brabham-Ford #29 slot is now represented in the full-base roster and covers his round-7 Brabham appearance.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant John Miles (lotus+ford, rounds 5-6,8-9,11) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Al Pease (eagle+climax, rounds 9) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Johnny Servoz-Gavin (matra+ford, rounds 7,9-11) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.

Coverage: Bill Brack has no dedicated source livery; his round-9 BRM stint is represented through the #22 G. Eaton livery proxy, retaining f1db-derived race/qualifying pace and inheriting that livery's reliability.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant John Cordts (brabham+climax, rounds 9) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Pedro Rodríguez (brm+brm, rounds 1-3) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Hans Herrmann (lotus+ford, rounds 7) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Rolf Stommelen (lotus+ford, rounds 7) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.

Coverage: Ernesto 'Tino' Brambilla is represented at round 8 through the source XML's Ferrari #11 Monza driver-swap block. f1db records him DNS ('Car raced by Rodríguez'), so this is source-full-grid fidelity rather than a claim that he started the race.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Xavier Perrot (brabham+ford, rounds 7) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Sam Tingle (brabham+repco, rounds 1) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant John Love (lotus+ford, rounds 1) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.
Coverage: f1db 1969 entrant Basil van Rooyen (mclaren+ford, rounds 1) has no livery in the referenced skinpack/AI set and is not included in this pack.

v1.2.0 source parity: F-Vintage_Gen2.xml (SHA-256 E52C3C35FA237A80013C520E93B27C3B18BAE4BE888D2BE5C93A9DF0E9AAE341) now supplies all 13 rating dimensions and vehicle_reliability for every one of its 26 base liveries. Piers Courage and Richard Attwood restore the two previously unused source slots; Brambilla carries the complete Monza swap block; Bill Brack remains the sole f1db-pace proxy and inherits Eaton #22 reliability. These staging changes affect new careers only.
Per-round variant file F-Vintage_Gen2_01Kyalami.xml maps to round 1 (South African Grand Prix): a composition-only subset of the base file (19 of 26 drivers, no rating changes).
Per-round variant file F-Vintage_Gen2_02Silverstone.xml maps to round 6 (British Grand Prix): a composition-only subset of the base file (19 of 26 drivers, no rating changes).
Per-round variant file F-Vintage_Gen2_03Nordschleiffe.xml maps to round 7 (German Grand Prix): a composition-only subset of the base file (22 of 26 drivers, no rating changes).
Per-round variant file F-Vintage_Gen2_04WatkinsGlen.xml maps to round 10 (United States Grand Prix): a composition-only subset of the base file (20 of 26 drivers, no rating changes).

Per-track source blocks remain absolute partial patches at Monaco (Beltoise and Surtees) and Silverstone (Surtees). The Monza block is represented as the explicit Amon-to-Brambilla same-livery entry swap, including its complete ratings and vehicle reliability, rather than as an override on Amon.

The Monza source block renames Ferrari #11 to Ernesto 'Tino' Brambilla. f1db records Brambilla DNS because Pedro Rodríguez raced the car, so the authored round-8 slot is source-full-grid fidelity rather than a historical-starter claim.

F-Vintage_Gen2_05Full.xml is byte-identical to the base file; F-Vintage_Gen2_06RegularF1.xml is jusk's default 18-regular grid. The full-base playability policy fields every available source slot until an authored exit: Piers appears in the regular subsets, while Attwood appears only in the Nordschleife subset but remains available throughout the full-base career roster. Monaco's 25-car track cap trims the 26 candidate slots at staging.

Per-round composition variants remain documentation-only: 01Kyalami, 02Silverstone, 03Nordschleiffe, and 04WatkinsGlen contain rating-identical subsets of the base file, so importing them would discard full-base roster depth without adding rating evidence.
